title: FINRA Case 25-02347 — Alex Stalkfleet v. Robinhood Financial, LLC
text:
Award
FINRA Dispute Resolution Services
In the Matter of the Arbitration Between:
Claimant Case Number: 25-02347
Alex Stalkfleet
vs.
Respondent Hearing Site: Phoenix, Arizona
Robinhood Financial LLC
Awards are rendered by independent arbitrators who are chosen by the parties to issue final,
binding decisions. FINRA makes available an arbitration forum—pursuant to rules approved by
the SEC—but has no part in deciding the award.
Nature of the Dispute: Customer vs. Member
This matter proceeded pursuant to Rule 12800 of the Code of Arbitration Procedure (“Code”).
Claimant did not request a hearing.
REPRESENTATION OF PARTIES
Claimant Alex Stalkfleet (“Claimant”) appeared pro se.
For Respondent Robinhood Financial LLC (“Respondent”): Lawton R. Graves, Esq., Murphy &
Anderson, P.A., Jacksonville, Florida.
CASE INFORMATION
Statement of Claim filed on or about: October 27, 2025.
Claimant’s Reply to Respondent’s Statement of Answer filed on or about: February 3, 2026.
Claimant’s Supplemental Written Response filed on or about: February 12, 2026.
Claimant’s Final Submission filed on or about: February 23, 2026.
Claimant signed the Submission Agreement: October 27, 2025.
Statement of Answer filed by Respondent on or about: December 19, 2025
Respondent’s Final Submission filed on or about: February 23, 2026.
Respondent signed the Submission Agreement: December 22, 2025.
CASE SUMMARY
In the Statement of Claim, Claimant asserted the following causes of action: unauthorized
trading; failure to supervise; account mismanagement; and platform/system failures. The causes
of action relate to CFTC-regulated derivatives/futures.

FINRA Dispute Resolution Services
Arbitration No. 25-02347
Award Page 2 of 3
Unless specifically admitted in the Statement of Answer, Respondent denied the allegations made
in the Statement of Claim and asserted various affirmative defenses.
RELIEF REQUESTED
In the Statement of Claim, Claimant requested:
1. Reimbursement of $36,533.00 in losses from unauthorized trades;
2. Prejudgment interest at a reasonable rate, not less than 6% per annum, from the date of
each unauthorized trade until payment in full;
3. Compensation for any losses, fees, or account restrictions resulting from Respondent’s
actions;
4. Full production of internal records, order confirmations, IP logs, and investigation notes
relating to Claimant’s account;
5. Reimbursement of the FINRA filing fee incurred by Claimant; and
6. Any additional relief the Arbitrator deems appropriate under FINRA rules.
In the Statement of Answer, Respondent requested:
1. Denial of the Statement of Claim, with prejudice, together with an award of all reasonable
forum costs and expenses; and
2. Other relief as the Arbitrator deems just and proper.
OTHER ISSUES CONSIDERED AND DECIDED
The Arbitrator acknowledges having read the pleadings and other materials filed by the parties.
AWARD
After considering the pleadings, the Arbitrator has decided and determined in full and final
resolution of the issues submitted for determination as follows:
1. Claimant’s claims are denied in their entirety.
2. FINRA Dispute Resolution Services shall retain the $600.00 filing fee that Claimant deposited
previously.
3. Any and all claims for relief not specifically addressed herein are denied.
FEES
Pursuant to the Code:
Respondent has paid to FINRA Dispute Resolution Services the $975.00 Member Surcharge
and $2,275.00 Member Process Fee previously invoiced.

FINRA Dispute Resolution Services
Arbitration No. 25-02347
Award Page 3 of 3
ARBITRATOR
Jeffrey L. Malek - Sole Public Arbitrator
I, the undersigned Arbitrator, do hereby affirm that I am the individual described herein and who
executed this instrument, which is my award.
Arbitrator's Signature
Jeffrey L. Malek 03/27/2026
Jeffrey L. Malek Signature Date
Sole Public Arbitrator
